untranslatable_reason: This signal is a meta-rule about evidence evaluation bias rather than a price-action pattern. It requires tracking win-rate statistics (min_disconfirming_ratio, required_out_of_sample_winrate, max_confirmatory_threshold, rolling 50-trade window performance) which are portfolio/pattern-level metrics, not bar-level indicator conditions. The backtest engine must implement statistical validation logic external to indicator-based entry/exit rules. Specific requirements: (1) Pattern win-rate >= 55% on out-of-sample data; (2) Disconfirming evidence >= 50% of pattern occurrences; (3) Reject if confirmatory cases <= 60%; (4) Exit if rolling 50-trade win rate < 52%; (5) Disable if disconfirming evidence >= 45% of recent occurrences. These cannot be expressed as simple indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: Signal ARONSON_DRY_0_0 encodes a cognitive bias correction framework requiring statistical hypothesis testing and evidence weighting at the pattern portfolio level. No valid entry/exit indicator conditions can be mechanically derived from this rule. A backtest harness would need to: (1) Track all pattern occurrences and outcomes in a dedicated statistics module; (2) Implement quarterly evidence reviews comparing confirming vs disconfirming cases; (3) Dynamically enable/disable patterns based on accumulated evidence ratios; (4) Monitor rolling win-rate windows to enforce minimum 52% threshold. This is architectural meta-logic, not a tradeable signal.
